description: Truth promotion boundary rules governing state transitions between AMBIG, NEAR, and OK

AMBIG_to_NEAR.description: Promote an AMBIG claim to NEAR truth state
requirements: New evidence must be submitted addressing the ambiguity
At least 1 fresh witness must provide CONFIRM attestation
Evidence hash must differ from original submission
Cooldown of 3 epochs since last promotion attempt must have elapsed

NEAR_to_OK.description: Promote a NEAR claim to OK truth state
requirements: An upgrade plan must be submitted and approved by at least 1 steward
At least 2 fresh witnesses must provide CONFIRM attestation
No active disputes against the claim
Cooldown of 5 epochs since NEAR certification must have elapsed
Upgrade plan must reference specific improvements over original submission
NEAR_to_OK.payout_effect: Remaining 0.618 of payout is released (total becomes 1.0)

OK_to_NEAR.description: Demote an OK claim to NEAR due to disputes
OK_to_NEAR.trigger: 3 or more DISPUTE attestations from distinct witnesses
effect: Excess payout (0.618 portion) is clawed back

NEAR_to_AMBIG.description: Demote a NEAR claim to AMBIG due to evidence invalidation
NEAR_to_AMBIG.trigger: Evidence hash matches a known-invalid hash in the invalidation registry
effect: Vested NEAR payout (0.382 portion) is clawed back
Player notified of demotion and reason

boundary_invariants: No claim may skip states (e.g., AMBIG cannot go directly to OK)
Promotion cooldowns are enforced strictly by epoch count
Fresh witnesses must not have witnessed the same claim in any prior state
Demotion triggers are evaluated continuously, not only at promotion time
Clawback amounts are computed from the original payout matrix values
All promotion and demotion events are logged in the ReceiptRegistry

void_rules.description: VOID is a terminal state with special rules
rules: VOID claims cannot be promoted to any other state
VOID is assigned when evidence is proven fraudulent or fabricated
VOID assignment requires unanimous witness DISPUTE (all witnesses)
VOID claims trigger ethics watchlist review for the submitting player
VOID claims remain in the registry for audit purposes but carry zero payout
